title: Forge project lockfile (<activeRoot>/forge.lock)
description: The RESOLVED per-project COMPOSITION manifest: the project analogue of package-lock.json. `forge.lock` is the single, git-committable statement of exactly what this project has composed — the ADOPTED set (.forge/composition.json, ADR-0019) JOINED with the TAILORING overlays (.forge/tailoring.json, ADR-0021), the ADJUDICATION choices (.forge/adjudication.json, ADR-0020), and each entry's pinned version/commit (the catalog record / .forge/sources.lock), plus a DETERMINISTIC content hash over the resolved entries (ADR-0022). It lives at the ACTIVE PROJECT ROOT (<activeRoot>/forge.lock), NOT under .forge/ and NOT in the git-tracked library, and is intended to be COMMITTED (like package-lock.json). It is DISTINCT from .forge/sources.lock (forge.sources.lock.v1, catalog.md §2.2), which pins each SOURCE's resolved git commit, lives under .forge/, and is machine-local (never committed): forge.lock records what the PROJECT resolved, sources.lock records where the SOURCE bytes came from. forge.lock CONSUMES sources.lock's per-entry commit as one input; that is the only relationship. The lockfile is DERIVED — a pure JOIN over existing per-project stores, reusing the read helpers of manager/compose.mjs, manager/tailor.mjs, and manager/conflict.mjs — so it adds no new authoritative state and is reproducible from its inputs. Owned by manager/lock.mjs (forge lock show|write|diff). `lock write` is MANIFEST-ONLY: it writes ONLY this file (writeJsonAtomic via manager/lib/store.mjs, preview by default, written on --apply); it MUST NOT materialize/generate or modify any real .claude/ file, the git-tracked library, or any resource content (materializing the composition into .claude/ is the bootstrap composer's job, OUT OF SCOPE — ADR-0022 §7).

schema.description: On-disk schema tag for this file.

_note.description: Free-form authoring note; ignored by the lock operator.

generatedAt.description: An ISO-8601 timestamp from the CLI runtime clock recording when the lock was last written (the standard JS Date API is available in the CLI itself; only Workflow scripts forbid it). Recorded for humans ONLY — it is the ONE field the content `hash` EXCLUDES, so the SAME composition yields the SAME hash across machines and times (ADR-0022 §3). MUST NOT feed the hash.

hash.description: A DETERMINISTIC content digest (sha256 via node:crypto, first 8–16 hex) over the CANONICAL resolved `entries` — entries sorted by uid then sourceId, each entry's overlays sorted, taken over the resolved fields (uid, sourceId, kind, version, commit, sorted overlays, adjudication) and EXCLUDING `generatedAt`. The SAME composition yields the SAME hash; re-writing an unchanged composition is idempotent (same entries → same hash). Two checkouts / a CI run compare this one short digest to assert they resolved the same thing (ADR-0022 §3).

entries.description: The resolved set: one entry per composed (uid, sourceId) in the project's composition (ADR-0019), with its tailoring overlays (ADR-0021), adjudication winner (ADR-0020), and pinned version/commit JOINED in. This is the resolved WHOLE; the composition, tailoring, and adjudication stores remain the irreducible inputs. Order is not significant; the operator sorts entries deterministically (by uid, then sourceId) and sorts each entry's overlays before computing the hash.

lockEntry.description: One resolved composition entry, identified by the pair (uid, sourceId) — the SAME identity the composition (ADR-0019) and tailoring (ADR-0021) stores use. It JOINS the adopted set with the resource kind/version, the pinned source commit, the tailoring overlays, and the adjudication winner. The same uid adopted from the library (sourceId === null) and from a source are two DISTINCT entries.

version.description: The resolved version. A `pin` tailoring overlay (ADR-0021) wins; else the catalog record version. May be null when neither the record nor a pin supplies one.

commit.description: The pinned source git commit (sha) JOINED from .forge/sources.lock (forge.sources.lock.v1, catalog.md §2.2 / ADR-0017) — the one input forge.lock consumes from the source lockfile — or null for a library-local entry (sourceId === null) or an unpinned source.

overlays.description: The tailoring overlays recorded for this (uid, sourceId) in .forge/tailoring.json (ADR-0021), folded in for the resolved manifest. Sorted (by type, then detail) before the content hash is computed.

adjudication.description: The recorded conflict-adjudication winner for this uid (ADR-0020): the sourceId chosen to win when the uid had >= 2 candidates, or null when there was no conflict / the library-local copy won / no choice is recorded.

oneOf.description: The winning sourceId for this uid (a recorded human T2 pick, ADR-0020).
No adjudication (no conflict, or the library-local copy, or no recorded choice).

overlay.description: One tailoring overlay folded into the resolved entry — a { type, detail } recorded in .forge/tailoring.json (ADR-0021 / BR-CAT-014). `type` is a closed set; `detail` is a short type-specific string (MAY be "" for fork/disable). See schemas/tailoring.schema.json for the authoritative overlay shape.

type.description: The overlay kind: pin (lock a version) | override (change a frontmatter field, "field → value") | layer (layer a fragment on top of the body) | gate (conditional activation) | fork (detach the body for local edits) | disable (turn the resource off).

detail.description: The type-specific overlay detail (e.g. "v3.2.0" for pin, "model → opus" for override). MAY be an empty string for fork/disable.

kind.description: The singular registry kind of the resource (JOINED from the catalog record, ADR-0005).

uid.description: A resource uid "<kind>:<id>": the singular registry kind (agent|skill|command|rule|hook|bundle|validator|mcp|meta-test|engine) and the resource id, joined by a single colon (ADR-0005 / the registry record).

sourceId.description: The source the resource was adopted from: the source id (manifests/sources.json#sources[].id), or null for the library-local copy (an owned in-tree record, source === null, ADR-0017).
